page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor. It features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by DDR5 memory totaling 192.0 GB with a speed of 6 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations rely on network-attached Elastic Block Store volumes, for which the instance is optimized. It also lacks a dedicated GPU, which limits its suitability for hardware-accelerated graphics or heavy parallel GPU computing. However, it provides a high baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the m8azn.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. Its stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance ranks in the top 10%, supported by advanced vector extensions like AVX-512 and cryptography flags such as AES and SHA_NI. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier results, reaching 529.59 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top 10%, with Redis achieving 176.14 million operations per second. For language model tasks, the instance processes 356.52 tokens per second for text generation on a small 135M model and 10.68 tokens per second on a large 70B model.

Given the high-frequency 5.0 GHz CPU and DDR5 memory, this instance offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy general-purpose workloads that require high single-core performance without the overhead of a GPU. It is suited for workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. This hardware profile delivers high resource density and processing efficiency, making it suitable for demanding workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 5.0 GHz, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and memory performance.

tagline: A high-frequency, dedicated AMD EPYC instance featuring DDR5 memory and 100 Gbps networking for demanding compute and database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 physical cores operating at 5.0 GHz
192 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Top-tier benchmark performance in CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
Suited for software compilation, database hosting, and CPU-based LLM inference
